behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Real failure depends on actual speech-to-text model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation). A stubbed transcript would hide diarization failures (MOCK-HIDE). No real OS primitive is the core risk; the core risk is AI output correctness (LLM-DEP-style rule applies to real model output). I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the behavior text focuses on a single model stage: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels"; it does not require validating multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API and assert speaker labels are preserved and mapped per utterance in the downstream summary input contract.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is parser/transform logic on transcript text to structured fields, and can fail entirely within one local code boundary. No required real network/OS/subprocess. No dependence on real model judgment if transcript is fixture-driven. I considered Integration, but rejected it because the behavior text is about extraction logic itself ("Transcript parsing extracts...") rather than cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript strings into the parsing function and assert exact assignee/task extraction, including edge cases (multiple assignees, ambiguous verbs, malformed lines).

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real summarization model decides to include for each attendee; deterministic stubs would always pass and miss omission failures (MOCK-HIDE, deterministic-assertion trap). I considered Workflow, but rejected it because this behavior can be validated at the single summarization decision boundary from transcript-to-summary; issue text says it "passes the transcript to an LLM to produce a structured summary."
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts with known attendee task assignments to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in the returned structured summary.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module boundary: decision-identification output must be handed to schema routing correctly (MOCK-MISS for cross-module handoff). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required for this behavior, (b) correctness does not depend on real model output because decisions are already "identified," (c) no multi-agentic chain required, (d) no planner→tool→critic-style LLM-stage orchestration in this behavior text. I considered Agentic, but rejected it due the disqualifying text "routes identified decisions" (routing after identification).
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision extractor output into the schema-mapping layer in-process and assert identified decisions land only in `decisions` fields, never in narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is a quality/placement claim about model output content; mocks replace model judgment and hide real failures (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). It is not only schema wiring because the requirement is that transcript-mentioned key decisions are actually surfaced correctly. I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the issue describes one summarization model step from transcript to structured output, not multiple agentic handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing explicit decisions through the production summarizer and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multiple sequential agentic steps (segment-by-segment processing plus merge), so MULTI-STEP applies directly. Coherence across merged segment outputs depends on real model behavior across steps; stubs can validate wiring but miss degradation at boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, force segmentation path, execute end-to-end segment transcription/summarization sequentially, and assert merged output is structurally complete and cross-segment decisions/action items remain coherent and non-duplicative.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end real run over multiple stages including at least two agentic steps (transcription model then summarization model) with sequential dependency, so Workflow is the true failure boundary. System concerns exist, but Workflow is higher because success also depends on chained model decisions and handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production pipeline on a real meeting file: upload, real speech-to-text call, real summarization call, and final structured delivery; assert completion status, schema validity, and no human retry/manual patching.
